focus_today:
**Franco, focus on this NOW:**

You have **TWO deadlines TODAY (Monday, 20 April)** — the MOW 217 P3 CAD submission and MSD 210 RAE21 assignment. Get these submitted before end of day, then shift to Quiz 8 prep (due Wednesday).

new_documents.summary: MOW 217 S1 2026 course contact and support information for Mechanical Engineering students, including module coordinator Isaac Setshedi and teaching assistants for CAD practicals and project mentoring.
key_requirements: Email Isaac Setshedi in advance to book consultation appointments
Contact appropriate teaching assistant based on group assignment (Groups 1-15: Devon Nell; CAD Practicals: Yanga Mkhize)
Attend tutorials/practicals on Thursdays for teaching assistant support
important_notes: Module Coordinator: Isaac Setshedi (isaac.setshedi@up.ac.za, 012 420 2014)
Consultation hours: Wed 10:30-11:20, Thu 13:30-14:20, Fri 10:30-11:20 (book via email)
Teaching Assistant office: Eng I Room 9-9, Thursday 10:30-17:20 available
Devon Nell is Project Mentor for Groups 1-15
WhatsApp contact available: 082 926 6380

new_documents.summary: Instructions for installing SolidWorks Student Premium edition (2025 version) on personal devices running Windows 10/11, with serial number provided. Installation is optional as SolidWorks is available in campus computer labs.
key_requirements: Download SolidWorks from www.solidworks.com/studentpremium
Use Student Premium Serial Number: 90200084810156779TQRZN9D
Install version 2025 (unless older version required)
Create 3DEXPERIENCE ID account using student email address
Access MySolidWorks at my.solidworks.com/account/student-access
Requires Windows 10 or 11 operating system
Requires strong internet connection and substantial download time
important_notes: Installation is NOT mandatory - SolidWorks available in Mining Study Center, Natural Sciences II, and Informatorium labs
CRITICAL: Only install SolidWorks PDM and SolidWorks Electrical if Microsoft SQL Server Instance is already created - failure to do so will prevent installation
Must use student email when creating 3DEXPERIENCE ID for certification accreditation
SolidWorks 2020 was last version compatible with Windows 7

new_documents.summary: YouTube playlist containing drawing lecture videos for MOW 217 S1 2026.
key_requirements: Access and review drawing lecture videos from provided playlist
important_notes: Video playlist link provided for self-study or lecture review

new_documents.summary: Semester Test 1 for MOW 217 on 18 March 2026 from 14:50 to 18:10, comprising a 180-minute CAD and drawing-based test with three parts (Part 1 on ClickUP, Parts 2-3 on UP A3 drawing sheets).
key_requirements: Submit all test files in PDF format
Part 1 completed on ClickUP (available in Tests and Exam/Semester Test 1 folder)
Parts 2 and 3 completed on UP A3 drawing sheet
Write computer and student numbers on yellow slips
Access additional files from H:/K: drive in folder mow217test1
Open ClickUP, SOLIDWORKS and read question paper in first 10 minutes
Submit files to ClickUP in last 10 minutes before 18:10
important_notes: Open-book test (print material allowed, no electronic media/general internet)
No students may enter after 30 minutes from start time (14:50)
No students may leave before 18:10 (end time)
Extra-time students start at 14:05 and 14:20
Printed question paper will be provided
Department representatives available to answer technical questions
ClickUP access permitted, but not general internet

new_documents.summary: Semester test 1 scheduled for Wednesday, 18 March 2026 from 14:50 to 18:10 (180 minutes). Venue allocation based on surname initial.
key_requirements: Arrive 10 minutes before session start time (14:40)
Locate assigned venue based on surname: A-Mb in Eng I MISC Computer Labs, Mc-Z in NSII Computer Labs 1-3
Students with extra time report to NSII Computer Lab 4 at 14:05 (15 min/hour) or 14:20 (10 min/hour)
Bring extra-time letter if applicable
important_notes: Test duration: 180 minutes
Venue allocation is by surname initial - know your assigned location
Extra time students must bring documentation to exam venue
Early arrival (10 min before) is mandatory

new_documents.summary: Semester Test 2 for MOW 217 scheduled for 09 May 2026 from 14:40 to 18:00, comprising 4 parts with specific submission requirements and venue rules.
key_requirements: Arrive 15 minutes early at test venue
Part 1 (FBD) - complete on provided paper sheet
Part 2 (quiz questions) - complete on ClickUP
Parts 3 & 4 - complete on UP A3 drawing sheet
Submit all files in PDF format via ClickUP
Save files regularly to H:/K:/T: drive (e.g., H:\mow217test2)
Write computer and student numbers on yellow slips
Cannot enter after 30 minutes from start time
Cannot leave before 18:00
important_notes: Test duration: 180 minutes (excluding 10 min setup and 10 min submission buffer)
Extra-time students start at 13:55 and 14:10
Open-book test - hardcopy materials and written/typed notes allowed; NO electronic media, USBs, cell phones, or laptops
ClickUP access available during test; general internet NOT allowed
Printed question paper will be provided
CAD drawing submission links and Parts 3 & 4 available on ClickUP under Tests and Exam/Semester Test 2 folder
Department representatives available to answer technical questions
Standard university late arrival and early departure rules apply

new_documents.summary: Semester test 2 scheduled for Saturday, 09 May 2026 from 14:40 to 18:00 (180 minutes) with venue allocation based on surname initial.
key_requirements: Arrive 10 minutes before 14:40 session start time
Identify correct venue based on surname first letter (A-Mb: Eng I MISC Computer Labs; Mc-Z: NSII Computer Labs 1-3)
Students with extra time report to NSII computer lab 4 (13:55 for 15 min/hour extra or 14:10 for 10 min/hour extra)

important_notes: Test duration is 180 minutes (3 hours)
Venue location varies by surname initial
Extra time students must bring documentation letter

new_documents.summary: Lecture on differential equations covering classification by type, order, and linearity, with emphasis on general and particular solutions, initial value problems, and verification methods applied to scientific and financial contexts.
key_requirements: Understand classification of differential equations by type, order, and linearity
Distinguish between general and particular solutions
Understand initial value problems
Learn verification methods for solutions
Apply concepts to scientific and financial applications
Watch introductory video: 1.1 Basic Ideas
important_notes: Video content is mandatory starting point for this lecture
Focus on both theoretical classification and practical problem-solving applications

new_documents.summary: This unit covers separable differential equations, initial value problems, existence and uniqueness theorems, and applications including natural growth, decay, and Newton's Law of Cooling.
key_requirements: Understand separable differential equations and variable isolation
Learn to solve initial value problems
Understand existence and uniqueness theorems
Apply models for natural growth and decay
Apply Newton's Law of Cooling
Watch the introductory video
important_notes: Video must be watched as a starting point for this unit
Focus on variable separation technique for integration
